title: Boat Rental in Xiamen: Costs, Seasons and What to Know
html:
Boat rental in Xiamen lets you sail the bays around Gulangyu Island and the Jiulong estuary, setting off from Wuyuan Bay Marina from roughly EUR 220 for a half-day. BoatHire24 connects you with local skippers and self-drive options across this subtropical Fujian port city.

Where to set off in Xiamen

Xiamen sits on a sheltered island coastline, so most trips begin in the calm inner harbour before opening towards the Taiwan Strait. The departure points below are easy to reach by metro or taxi.

  • Wuyuan Bay Marina: the main leisure-boating base on the eastern shore, close to the city centre.
  • Gulangyu Island: the car-free heritage island a short hop across the strait, framed by colonial villas.
  • Xiamen International Cruise Terminal: the deep-water gateway on the north-west coast.
  • Huandao Road and Baicheng Beach: the seafront promenade where many day trips anchor for swimming.
  • Jiulong River estuary: the broad river mouth towards Zhangzhou.
  • Dadeng Island: a quieter outlying island to the east, near the airport district.

Which boat to rent in Xiamen?

Choose a small motorboat for a half-day around Gulangyu, or a crewed motor yacht for a full day towards the outer islands. Your pick depends on group size, budget and whether you drive yourself or sit back with a skipper.

  1. Small motorboat: ideal for a quick harbour spin or fishing, seats up to 6 guests, from EUR 220 a half-day.
  2. RIB or speedboat: best for fast hops to Gulangyu and beach stops, seats up to 8, from EUR 320 a half-day.
  3. Sailing catamaran: stable and roomy for families, seats up to 12, from EUR 650 a full day.
  4. Crewed motor yacht: for sunset cruising, seats up to 12, from EUR 1,100 a day. To picture the upper end, compare the Mefasa 90, a 90-foot yacht with a larger crewed deck.
  5. Traditional pleasure junk: a slow scenic option for the inner harbour, seats up to 10, from EUR 480 a day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Xiamen?

Prices vary with boat size, season and crew, but the bands below give a realistic planning figure for 2026. When you are ready to hire a boat through BoatHire24, the live listing confirms the exact rate, deposit and fuel policy.

  • A shared sightseeing tour around Gulangyu costs from EUR 25 per person.
  • A small self-drive motorboat for a half-day starts near EUR 220.
  • A sailing catamaran for a full day ranges from roughly EUR 650 to EUR 1,000.
  • A crewed yacht with skipper typically starts at EUR 1,100 per day and rises with length.

When is the best season to sail in Xiamen?

The calmest, warmest sailing runs from April to October, with the peak months falling between June and September. Winters are mild but breezier, and the city sits within the East Asian monsoon belt.

Late summer brings the highest sea temperatures, though it overlaps with the regional typhoon window. The local southerly monsoon wind, the nanfeng, builds through the warmer months and shapes afternoon swell along the open coast.

Plan island hops for the morning: the nanfeng often freshens after midday in high summer, so an early start towards Gulangyu usually means flatter water and a more comfortable crossing of roughly 1 nautical mile from Wuyuan Bay.

How does booking work?

You reserve online, confirm the date with the operator, then meet the boat at the agreed marina. Most listings show included extras, cancellation terms and what to bring. If you are unsure about deposits, the common questions about booking page walks through each step before you pay.

At a glance

  • Price band: from EUR 220 half-day to EUR 1,100-plus per crewed day
  • Peak months: June to September
  • Typical trip length: 4 hours half-day, 8 hours full day
  • Capacity: 6 to 12 guests on most leisure boats
  • Currency: euros (EUR) for indicative pricing, settled locally
  • Main base: Wuyuan Bay Marina, Xiamen

Questions about boat rental in Xiamen

Do I need a licence to rent a boat in Xiamen?

For a self-drive boat in China you generally need a recognised boating qualification, while crewed charters require none from guests. The operator confirms the exact documents accepted for your chosen vessel before departure.

Can I visit Gulangyu Island by private boat?

You can cruise the waters around Gulangyu and anchor offshore, but landing is regulated to protect the UNESCO heritage zone. Your skipper will advise on permitted stops and the best photo angles from the strait.

How many people fit on a typical charter?

Most leisure boats in Xiamen carry between 6 and 12 guests depending on type, with motorboats suiting couples and catamarans suiting families. Always confirm the legal capacity on the listing you book.

What happens during typhoon season?

If a typhoon warning is issued during the summer peak, trips are postponed or refunded under operator terms rather than sailing into rough seas. Flexible dates are wise for August bookings.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Xiamen or list your own, from a self-drive motorboat to a crewed yacht. If you own a vessel berthed along the Fujian coast, you can rent out your boat and reach travellers exploring the strait.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Xiamen and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
